steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence uses the correlative pair "not only... but also." Standard usage requires no extra commas or dashes splitting "not only X but also Y" — both parts are part of one continuous predicate.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
"may be caused not only by cyclones but also by vapor streams" reads cleanly with no internal punctuation. A and B insert dashes that don't pair with anything; C wraps "not only by cyclones" in commas, which improperly isolates a non-parenthetical phrase.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
